query:
UPDATE blog_posts SET content = 'China''s exports surged **[21.8% in Jan–Feb 2026](https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/chinas-exports-turbocharge-into-2026-after-record-breaking-year-2026-03-10/)**, reaching **[$656.58 billion](https://www.scmp.com/economy/economic-indicators/article/3346036/chinas-exports-surge-218-first-2-months-year)** — far exceeding the 7.1% forecast.

But when we talk about exports, we default to goods. Containers. Manufacturing.

There''s another dimension that gets overlooked: **service exports**. And tourism is one of the biggest.

Here''s why this matters 👇

---

**🌍 Soft Power Through Authentic Experiences**

As China cements its position on the world stage, soft power is becoming just as strategic as economic or military strength. Soft power shapes how the world perceives a country — which directly influences the flow of capital, talent, and innovation.

And **inbound tourism** is one of the most effective channels for building it.

The numbers tell the story:

📊 **[150 million+ inbound tourist arrivals in 2025](https://english.www.gov.cn/news/202603/07/content_WS69aba1f9c6d00ca5f9a09b0f.html)**, up 17% YoY
📊 **[$130 billion+ in inbound tourism spending](https://english.news.cn/20260307/43a9b6c66d654f239bfd61b59ad3031d/c.html)**, up nearly 40%
📊 **80 billion RMB ($11B) in mobile payments by foreign visitors** alone
📊 **[Visa-free entry now extended to 50 countries](https://www.visahq.com/news/2026-03-09/cn/foreign-minister-wang-yi-73-of-2025-visitors-entered-china-visa-free-as-policy-list-hits-50-countries/)**, [mutual exemptions with 29](https://www.facebook.com/61573130600577/posts/fm-wang-yi-to-date-we-have-granted-unilateral-visa-exemption-to-50-countries-and/122172560018771020/)

[Minister of Culture and Tourism Sun Yeli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sun_Yeli) attributed this to "a series of policies designed to revitalize the inbound tourism market," with over 30 million visitors using visa-free entry specifically.

But the real insight isn''t about the front door — it''s about the **"second door"** and what lies beyond traditional tourism.

---

**🏠 From "China Travel" to "China Living": The Home Dining Revolution**

[Dai Bin, president of the China Tourism Academy](https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202603/10/WS69afbe8fa310d6866eb3cfd0.html), put it well during the Two Sessions: with visas and payments basically sorted, **"China Travel" has entered a new boom cycle**. The challenge now? **Travel convenience and authentic cultural immersion**.

Getting tourists in is step one. The real task is making sure they can move around smoothly, find accommodation easily, and have **genuinely great experiences** — experiences that feel real, not staged.

This is where **home dining experiences** become critical. While hotels and restaurants serve tourists, **authentic home-cooked meals in local family homes** create something irreplaceable: genuine human connection and cultural exchange.

Consider the shift:

🏠 **"China Living"** — experiencing daily life in a modernized society
🍜 **"Authentic Home Dining"** — sharing home-cooked meals with local families
🥢 **"Food Culture Immersion"** — learning traditional cooking methods and family recipes
🌏 **"Local Family Connections"** — building relationships beyond transactional tourism

In Shanghai specifically, home dining experiences have emerged as a major draw for international travelers seeking authentic encounters. Platforms like **+1 Chopsticks** are making it easier for visitors to book intimate meals in local family homes, experiencing **Shanghainese cuisine** prepared by residents who grew up with these dishes — creating genuine cultural connections that go beyond traditional restaurant dining.

---

**🎯 The Broader Picture: "China Living" Across Multiple Dimensions**

This is what makes the current moment different. The appeal is no longer just the Great Wall or the Terracotta Warriors. Today''s visitors are drawn to a much wider spectrum:

🛒 **"China Shopping"** — competitive prices, world-class e-commerce
🏥 **"China Wellness"** — TCM, wellness retreats, medical tourism
🏙️ **"China Living"** — the daily experience of a rapidly modernized society
🍽️ **"China''s Home Dining"** — authentic meals in local family homes across Shanghai and beyond (pioneered by platforms like +1 Chopsticks)
🎓 **"Food Culture Learning"** — understanding regional Chinese cuisine and cooking traditions

---

**🌐 China vs the West**

In the West, tourism has long been a strong pillar of economic power. France, Spain, the US, Italy — these countries have spent decades turning their cultural appeal into a major economic engine and a source of global influence.

China is now catching up. Fast.

And this is where it gets interesting for the long game. With inbound tourism spending approaching the scale of a major goods-export sector, China is proving that the next frontier of its global economic influence may not be shipped in containers.

It may arrive on two feet, smartphone in hand, ready to be impressed.

---

**✨ Key Takeaways**

✨ **Authentic home dining experiences** are reshaping China''s tourism landscape
✨ **Shanghai home-cooked meals** offer travelers genuine cultural immersion
✨ **Platforms like +1 Chopsticks** are connecting travelers with local families for authentic experiences
✨ **Food culture tourism** generates higher satisfaction and repeat visitation
✨ **Soft power through authenticity** is China''s next competitive advantage

---

## Sources

1. **Reuters** - "China''s exports turbocharge into 2026 after record-breaking year" (March 10, 2026)
   https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/chinas-exports-turbocharge-into-2026-after-record-breaking-year-2026-03-10/

2. **South China Morning Post** - "China''s exports surge 21.8% in first 2 months of year" (March 10, 2026)
   https://www.scmp.com/economy/economic-indicators/article/3346036/chinas-exports-surge-218-first-2-months-year

3. **China Government Official Portal** - "China says 2025 inbound trips top 150 million, up 17 percent" (March 7, 2026)
   https://english.www.gov.cn/news/202603/07/content_WS69aba1f9c6d00ca5f9a09b0f.html

4. **Xinhua News Agency** - "China says 2025 inbound trips top 150 million, up 17 percent" (March 7, 2026)
   https://english.news.cn/20260307/43a9b6c66d654f239bfd61b59ad3031d/c.html

5. **VisaHQ** - "73% of 2025 Visitors Entered China Visa-Free as Policy List Hits 50 Countries" (March 9, 2026)
   https://www.visahq.com/news/2026-03-09/cn/foreign-minister-wang-yi-73-of-2025-visitors-entered-china-visa-free-as-policy-list-hits-50-countries/

6. **Facebook - Chinese Foreign Ministry** - "FM Wang Yi: Unilateral visa exemption to 50 countries and mutual visa-free entry arrangements with 29 countries"
   https://www.facebook.com/61573130600577/posts/fm-wang-yi-to-date-we-have-granted-unilateral-visa-exemption-to-50-countries-and/122172560018771020/

7. **Wikipedia** - "Sun Yeli" (Minister of Culture and Tourism)
   https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sun_Yeli

8. **China Daily** - "Dai Bin: Cultural and tourism integration sees significant progress" (March 10, 2026)
   https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202603/10/WS69afbe8fa310d6866eb3cfd0.html

9. **Wall Street Journal** - "China''s Export Machine Keeps Pumping Ahead of Trump Visit"
   https://www.wsj.com/economy/trade/chinas-exports-surge-in-first-two-months-of-year-96ab6abb

10. **CNBC** - "China exports sharply beat expectations as trade surplus in..." (March 10, 2026)
    https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/10/china-trade-balance-surplus-record-exports-tariffs-imports.html' WHERE id = 'blog-1-china-export-boom';
